methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $730M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
ICEYE is a Series E-stage space technology company founded by Rafał Modrzewski and Pekka Laurila, currently valued at €10.5B (~$12B) and having raised $730M+ to date. The broader Space & Aerospace sector includes 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX and Planet Labs, though direct same-stage peer data is sparse.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology given data constraints. Using total capital raised ($730M) as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.4x multiplier typical of Series E-to-growth-stage step-ups yields an implied valuation of $1.0B. This produces a triangulated range of $869M–$1.2B (low-to-high), with $1.0B as the median point. Public Space & Aerospace comparables trade at a 25.8x median P/S multiple across the six tickers identified, but cannot be applied without ICEYE's disclosed revenue figure—standard for private companies.

The $1.0B implied valuation sits substantially below the stated current round valuation of $12B. This 12x gap reflects either: material revenue scale and growth metrics not present in available data; significant recent operational progress post-raise; or valuation inflation relative to fundamentals-based methods. The single-method reliance (stage-progression) and absence of narrow peer benchmarks represent material analytical constraints. Resolution requires granular revenue, gross margin, and unit economics data.
